book_claims.claim: Listed as a source of omega-3 ALA (0.31 g per 1 tsp) and vitamin E (1.1 mg per 1 tbsp, 6% DV)
book_claims.mechanism: Plant oil with ALA omega-3 and vitamin E

supplementary_research.finding: Soybean oil has an omega-6 to omega-3 ratio of ~7:1; excessive use relative to olive oil may promote inflammation; canola or walnut oil provide better omega-3 balance
Soybean oil vitamin E (primarily gamma-tocopherol) has antioxidant properties; however, high linoleic acid content may increase lipid peroxidation at cooking temperatures

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Plant form; ~5–15% conversion to EPA/DHA; lower efficacy than marine omega-3s
Very high omega-6; unfavorable ratio when used as primary cooking oil
Primarily gamma-tocopherol; antioxidant; degrades with high-heat cooking

synergies.description: Olive oil is the preferred cooking fat in Longevity Diet; soybean oil's high omega-6 content makes it a less favorable alternative
ALA in soybean oil contributes modestly to omega-3 status; best used sparingly alongside marine omega-3 sources
Both are plant-based omega-3 containing oils; walnut oil has a more favorable omega-6:omega-3 ratio
